Class.name: OAuth2.Server.Validate
Class.Description: PYD20191122: A Copy of %OAuth2.Server.Validate to Customize the ValidateUser Entry point and call an LDAP / Active Directory server. The %OAuth2.Server.Validate class is the default ValidateUserClass which is included with the server. The default class will use the user database of the Cache instance where the authorization server is located to validate the user. The supported properties will be issuer (Issuer), roles and sub (Username). The ValidateUserClass is specified in the Authorization Server Configuration.

Parameter.name: AdminDN
Parameter.Description: LDAP User to Scan/Read the Directory

Parameter.name: AdminPassword
Parameter.Description: Credentials for the LDAP Read user — replace via System Default Settings

Parameter.name: LDAPServer2
Parameter.Description: Secondary LDAP Server. Not yet used.

Parameter.name: FILTER
Parameter.Description: The attribute to search on (AD: sAMAccountname; OpenLDAP: uid)

Parameter.name: UnixCertificateFile
Parameter.Description: The Certificate file for SSL/TLS (Unix). Enables StartTLS when non-empty.

Method.name: ValidateUser
Method.Description: PYD20191122: Customised ValidateUser - calls out to an LDAP Server. All the LDAP Code has been copied from [SAMPLES]LDAP.MAC; look there for full comments. Return 1 if username/password is valid, 0 otherwise. If password="", the user is already logged into an existing session and the password does not need to be validated.
